# Blueprint v0.3.0 — open-source launch, license rip, in-app help

First release as an open-source project. Apache 2.0 — yours to read,
fork, deploy, modify. The marketing site at llmblueprint.ai is now
an OSS landing surface and the lead-gen funnel for Inspire AI Lab's
consulting practice.

## Headline changes since v0.2.3

### License-enforcement code removed
The first-launch "Personal vs Commercial" modal is gone. The 14-day
trial banner is gone. The "Enter license key" UI is gone. Blueprint
boots straight into the welcome overlay → dashboard. No nag, no
gate, no honor-system flow to navigate. All under Apache 2.0.

Removed packages: `internal/license`, `cmd/blueprint-license`,
`frontend/src/license`. Their absence is the breaking change that
justifies the 0.3.0 minor bump.

### In-app help — `?` icons on every major card
Click the `?` next to a card title in the desktop app to open the
corresponding how-to article on llmblueprint.ai/how-to in your
default browser. **23 articles** ship covering Plan, Hardware,
Deploy, Calibrate, Dashboard cards, Hosts, and Maintain. **14 cards**
in the desktop app are wired up so far:

  Prompt cache, Router, Service, Models on disk (local + remote),
  Inference chat, Python runtime, Verify chat, LoRA training,
  Remote servers, Hosts, Performance metrics, GPU breakdown,
  Reset confirmation modal.

Articles are MDX-hosted on the site so updates ship via `git push`
without a desktop release.

### OS keychain caching for remote svc bearer tokens
New `internal/secrets` package wraps `zalando/go-keyring` —
Windows Credential Manager / macOS Keychain / Linux Secret Service.
`svcclient.New` now takes a `TokenCache`; subsequent Connect calls
to the same host skip the SFTP roundtrip. On 401 we refetch via
SSH and update the cache. Cleared on host removal.

### Pull-to-remote
New `POST /v1/pull` + `GET /v1/pull?modelId=&quant=` on the svc.
`svcclient.Pull` / `svcclient.PullStatus`. `RemoteHostPull` /
`RemoteHostPullStatus` IPC. The Models card on the Dashboard now
has a "Pull to host" picker with an inline progress row — you can
download a GGUF directly onto a connected remote host without
SSHing in to run `huggingface-cli`.

### AMD ROCm + Apple Silicon GPU detection
`/v1/snapshot` now unions readings from `nvidia-smi`, `rocm-smi`
(Linux + AMD ROCm), and `system_profiler SPDisplaysDataType`
(macOS Apple Silicon). The marketing site already claimed to
support these platforms; the snapshot data now backs that up.

### Structured logging
New `internal/logging` package wrapping `log/slog`. Writes to
`~/.blueprint/logs/blueprint.log` (rotated to `.old` past 10 MB)
and stderr. Wired into svcapi auth-failure auditing — failed
bearer attempts now leave a structured WARN entry instead of
disappearing into the void.

### Inference chat — better contrast
Assistant (LLM) message bubble now has a `border-2 border-foreground/25`
and `bg-card` so it stands out against the chat-area background.
Chat area uses a `bg-muted/20` background with thicker top/bottom
borders so the conversation zone reads as distinct from the
sampling controls above and the input form below.

## Quality + polish

- **Context-leak fix** at `cmd/blueprint-svc/supervisor.go` —
  `WithCancel` was leaking one goroutine per child restart cycle;
  `go vet ./...` now clean.
- **Project-wide mojibake sweep** — 14 .go files had cp1252-decoded
  em-dashes, box-drawing chars, and arrows that survived a prior
  PowerShell round-trip. Two of them were user-visible error strings.
  All fixed at the byte level.
- **Doc-comment polish** — every package has a Go-convention
  contiguous package doc; every exported identifier has a doc
  comment; every Go file has an `// Author: Amar Mond.` attribution.
  `go doc ./...` renders cleanly.
- **README rewrite** — replaced the "Phase 1 scaffolding" placeholder
  with current v0.3.0 reality (multi-host fleet, OS keychain, GPU
  detection across vendors, pull-to-remote, etc.).
- **CONTRIBUTING.md** added with realistic "best-effort, custom
  asks → needs-consultation label" expectations for OSS contributors.

## Marketing site (blueprint-site, separate repo)

- Pivoted from "buy a license" to "Apache 2.0 open source" — home
  hero, download page, sitemap, footer all reframed
- Removed `/pricing`, `/buy`, `/api/billing` routes entirely
- Stripped the `stripe` dep + email-gating on `/download`
- Added `/how-to` index + 23 article pages

## Build artifacts

This release ships unsigned developer builds — code signing is
gated behind `WINDOWS_CERT` / `APPLE_DEVELOPER_ID` GitHub secrets
that haven't been provisioned yet. Practical impact:

- **Windows**: SmartScreen may show "Windows protected your PC."
  Click *More info* → *Run anyway*.
- **macOS**: Right-click the .app → Open → confirm the dialog.
  (Or `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine Blueprint.app` from terminal.)
- **Linux**: Just runs.

Signed builds are tracked separately for a future release.

## Upgrading from v0.2.x

- Existing `~/.blueprint/` data is preserved across the upgrade.
- The first launch after upgrading will no longer show the
  Personal/Commercial modal. Your existing `license.json` is
  ignored (we no longer read it). You can delete it manually if
  you want: `~/.blueprint/license.json`.
- If you had the Windows Service installed (`blueprint-svc.exe`),
  it keeps running. The new svc binary is shipped in the v0.3.0
  installer; the Service card's "Install service" path will install
  the new version side-by-side. Uninstall the old one first via the
  Service card if you want a clean upgrade.
